methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Machina Labs is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $80M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of six same-stage, same-sector private companies (1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat among them), plus one public comparable, spanning a wide range from $300M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression, implies a valuation of $152M. Peer-median analysis across the six comparable companies yields a median valuation of $1.1B, reflecting significant dispersion in the robotics sector at this stage. The peer range ($300M–$10.0B) underscores heterogeneity in investor appetite and perceived technology maturity within the cohort.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $129M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $614M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around market differentiation and capital efficiency relative to better-funded peers. Key variables include the company's technical defensibility versus 1X Technologies and Bedrock Robotics, burn rate relative to $80M deployed, and customer traction in core manufacturing applications. Further diligence on pipeline quality and gross margins would refine positioning within this range.
